post.content:
Coding 1: Course schedule 2 and little bit variations
Coding 2: Same as Task schedule (https://leetcode.com/problems/task-scheduler/)
Was able to solve it, however the follow up was to print all task. was not able to solve it.
How do we solve follow up?

Result: Failed

I have modified the code actually the code returns the time and in arr, I am storing the tasks which later will be printed. 
The logic is to maintain two min heaps, hea1 and hea2 also a global time (t), and pop all elements from hea2 till it satisfies time and push into hea1 and then check same for hea1 and always increase the timer.

Let me know if there are any doubts !!! or if my approach is wrong.
```
class Solution:
    def leastInterval(self, tasks: List[str], n: int) -> int:
        
        d = Counter(tasks)
        hea1 = [[i,j] for i,j in enumerate(sorted(d.keys(),key= lambda x: -d[x]))]
        heapify(hea1)
        hea2 = []
        t = 0
        nums = len(tasks)
        arr = []
        while nums:
            fl = 1
            while hea2 and hea2[0][0]<=t:
                heappush(hea1,heappop(hea2))
            if hea1 and hea1[0][0] <= t:
                x,y = heappop(hea1)
                d[y]-=1
                nums-=1
                arr.append(y)
                fl = 0
                if d[y] > 0:
                    heappush(hea2,[x+n+1,y])                    
            if fl:
                arr.append('idle')
            t+=1
        print(arr)
        return t
```

To print all tasks sequence, just have StringBuilder[topFreq], and fill it out with letters starting with top frequency until we run out.\nWhen we run out, fill out _ until all idle slots also run out.\nThen just print the strings.
how come u had two coding rounds ? I can see some people are having 2nd round as LLD one.
Was the constraints same in task schedule as the question given in leetcode?
